title: sophora tetraptera subsp. howinsula
intro: Sophora tetraptera subsp. howinsula, also known as the island kowhai, is a plant species endemic to the Kermadec Islands, a subantarctic archipelago northeast of New Zealand. The island kowhai has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Sophora tetraptera subsp. howinsula include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the island kowhai has been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, to support overall well-being, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
